title: Psychilis domingensis
intro: Psychilis domingensis, also known as the island bird's nest, is a terrestrial orchid native to the Caribbean and Central America. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Psychilis domingensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Psychilis domingensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of extracts and tinctures to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Psychilis domingensis is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Psychilis and is a flowering plant native to the Caribbean. Within the orchid family (Orchidaceae), Psychilis domingensis is part of the subtribe Cranichidinae. The subfamily Cranichidinae is itself part of the large family Orchidaceae. This classification places it within the larger family of orchids.
